shortDescription: IRCC work permit · Employer-sponsored · LMIA or exempt category

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current work permit fees on canada.ca. LMIA processing via ESDC adds significant time and employer cost.

alert.title: LMIA vs LMIA-exempt
alert.body: Most employer-specific permits require LMIA from ESDC unless exempt (ICT, CUSMA, PGWP, etc.). Verify category before quoting timeline.

about.label: Description
about.value: Employer-specific or open work permit allowing foreign nationals to work in Canada. LMIA-required streams need positive LMIA from Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC).

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Valid job offer from Canadian employer · LMIA or exempt category · Qualifying skills/experience · Medical/biometrics as required

about.label: LMIA process
about.value: Employer must advertise, recruit, and demonstrate no Canadians available. ESDC LMIA adds weeks to months before IRCC application.

about.label: After approval
about.value: Work for specified employer (closed permit) or open category. Maintain status; employer compliance with LMIA conditions.

eligibility.criterion: LMIA positive or exempt category confirmed

redFlagsBanner: If LMIA refused, address ESDC reasons before reapplication—do not submit IRCC work permit without positive LMIA when required.

redFlags.title: LMIA advertising deficiencies
redFlags.description: Employer did not meet recruitment advertising requirements.
redFlags.fix: Ensure ESDC advertising compliance before LMIA submit

redFlags.title: Wrong NOC/ wage offer
redFlags.description: Job classification or wage below median for region.
redFlags.fix: Verify TEER and prevailing wage on Job Bank

redFlags.title: Fake job offer
redFlags.description: Employer not genuine or employee-payback schemes.
redFlags.fix: Verify employer legitimacy; avoid fraudulent job offers

redFlags.title: Prior status violations
redFlags.fix: Address inadmissibility; legal counsel if needed

redFlags.title: Closed permit employer change
redFlags.description: Worker switching employers without new permit.
redFlags.fix: New LMIA and work permit for employer change

faqs.a: No. Both ESDC (LMIA) and IRCC must approve where applicable.
Varies—often 2–5 months for standard LMIA. Verify current ESDC service standards.
Includes ICT transfers, CUSMA professionals, PGWP, spousal open permits, etc.—verify exemption code.
Spouse may qualify for open work permit in some categories—verify current rules.
LMIA application fee CAD $1,000 per position; plus recruitment costs.
Some nationals may flagpole—verify eligibility and current policy.
Canadian experience may support CEC via Express Entry.
Review ESDC reasons; address recruitment or wage issues; reapply or explore exempt categories.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: Employer-specific or open work permit allowing foreign nationals to work in Canada. LMIA-required streams need positive LMIA from Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC).

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: Valid job offer from Canadian employer · LMIA or exempt category · Qualifying skills/experience · Medical/biometrics as required

faqs.q: What is LMIA process for this service?
faqs.a: Employer must advertise, recruit, and demonstrate no Canadians available. ESDC LMIA adds weeks to months before IRCC application.
Employer must advertise, recruit, and demonstrate no Canadians available. ESDC LMIA adds weeks to months before IRCC application.

faqs.a: ESDC (LMIA) · IRCC (work permit)

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: Work for specified employer (closed permit) or open category. Maintain status; employer compliance with LMIA conditions.

faqs.a: Valid job offer / contract is typically required.

faqs.q: Is LMIA positive or exempt category confirmed required?
faqs.a: Employer responsibility for LMIA
Qualifying work experience/education is typically required.
Assess whether Medical exam (if required) is satisfied for this case.
Assess whether Biometrics completed is satisfied for this case.
No criminal inadmissibility is typically required.
Verify employer legitimacy
3–9m — LMIA + IRCC combined
CAD $155 — Worker fee + biometrics

15 — + LMIA if applicable

faqs.a: Employer did not meet recruitment advertising requirements.

faqs.a: Verify TEER and prevailing wage on Job Bank

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
proTips: Confirm LMIA vs exempt at intake
Verify employer genuine and financially stable
Align NOC/TEER with actual job duties
Track LMIA and IRCC stages separately
postApproval: Send work permit conditions and employer name
Explain closed vs open permit rules

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Verify employer is not on IRCC non-compliant list before LMIA investment.

quiz.question: What is an employer-specific work permit?
options: A permit tied to a named employer and job conditions
quiz.explanation: Closed permits list employment conditions such as employer, role, location, and validity.

quiz.question: What does LMIA stand for?
quiz.explanation: An LMIA is an ESDC assessment used in many TFWP cases.

quiz.question: Which program commonly requires an LMIA?
quiz.explanation: The TFWP is generally LMIA-based.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of an LMIA?
options: To assess the labour market impact of hiring a foreign worker
quiz.explanation: ESDC reviews whether hiring the worker will have a positive or neutral impact.

quiz.question: What does IMP stand for?
quiz.explanation: The IMP includes LMIA-exempt work permit categories.

quiz.question: What does LMIA-exempt mean?
options: A work permit may be issued under a specific exemption code without an LMIA
quiz.explanation: LMIA-exempt does not necessarily mean work-permit exempt.

quiz.question: Who usually applies for an LMIA?
quiz.explanation: Employers apply to ESDC/Service Canada before the worker applies for the permit.

quiz.question: What supports a worker's application after a positive LMIA?
options: LMIA approval and employment offer details
quiz.explanation: The worker uses the LMIA and offer documents to support the permit request.

quiz.question: What is an open work permit?
options: A permit not tied to one specific employer
quiz.explanation: Open permits allow work for most employers, subject to conditions.

quiz.question: What is a typical closed-permit condition?
options: Work only for the employer named on the permit
quiz.explanation: Working outside listed conditions can create compliance problems.

quiz.question: What does TFWP stand for?
quiz.explanation: TFWP refers to LMIA-based temporary foreign worker hiring.

quiz.question: Who issues most work permits?
quiz.explanation: IRCC issues work permits while ESDC assesses LMIAs.

quiz.question: Who assesses most LMIA applications?
options: Employment and Social Development Canada or Service Canada
quiz.explanation: ESDC/Service Canada reviews labour market factors.

quiz.question: Which category can be LMIA-exempt?
quiz.explanation: Certain ICT cases may qualify under IMP rules if criteria are met.

quiz.question: What is a job offer's role?
options: It describes the proposed employment position and conditions
quiz.explanation: Work permit applications often rely on detailed employment terms.

quiz.question: What is the employer compliance fee linked to?
quiz.explanation: Employers often submit the offer and pay a fee through the Employer Portal.

quiz.question: Which portal is used for many LMIA-exempt offers?
quiz.explanation: The Employer Portal links the offer to the worker's application.

quiz.question: What does a foreign worker usually need before starting work?
quiz.explanation: Foreign nationals generally need work authorization unless an exemption applies.

quiz.question: What is not a work permit by itself?
quiz.explanation: A job offer supports an application but is not authorization to work.

quiz.question: What is a NOC code used for?
options: Classifying job duties and skill profile
quiz.explanation: NOC codes help classify occupations for immigration and labour programs.

quiz.question: Which pathway is most tied to recruitment evidence?
quiz.explanation: Many LMIA streams require recruitment and labour market evidence.

quiz.question: What does a positive LMIA generally indicate?
options: Hiring is expected to have a positive or neutral labour market impact
quiz.explanation: A positive LMIA can support an employer-specific work permit.

quiz.question: What does a negative LMIA usually mean?
options: It cannot support the requested work permit
quiz.explanation: A negative LMIA does not provide the needed labour market support.

quiz.question: Which worker is likely on a closed permit?
options: A cook authorized for one named restaurant
quiz.explanation: Employer-specific permits are tied to the listed employer and role.

quiz.question: Why read printed permit conditions?
options: They define what work is authorized
quiz.explanation: Working outside permit conditions can affect current and future status.

quiz.question: What is usually first in an LMIA-based hiring process?
options: The employer identifies the correct LMIA stream and recruitment requirements
quiz.explanation: The employer must satisfy stream-specific requirements before the worker applies.

quiz.question: Why are recruitment records important for LMIAs?
options: They show efforts to hire Canadians or permanent residents first
quiz.explanation: Recruitment evidence is central to many labour market assessments.

quiz.question: Which wage issue is commonly assessed in LMIAs?
options: Whether the offered wage meets prevailing wage rules
quiz.explanation: ESDC reviews wages for the occupation and location.

quiz.question: What does an employer submit for many IMP closed permits?
options: An offer of employment through the Employer Portal
quiz.explanation: Many LMIA-exempt employer-specific permits require an online employer submission.

quiz.question: Which number may the worker need from the Employer Portal?
quiz.explanation: This number links the employer submission to the worker's application.

quiz.question: What should be consistent across offer and application?
options: Title, duties, wage, employer, and location
quiz.explanation: Consistency helps officers assess the employment and category.

quiz.question: Why must the NOC match actual duties?
options: Officers assess duties, not job title alone
quiz.explanation: A title cannot overcome duties that do not fit the claimed NOC.

quiz.question: What is a transition plan?
options: An employer plan to reduce reliance on foreign workers
quiz.explanation: Some high-wage LMIA cases require transition planning.

quiz.question: What can trigger low-wage LMIA rules?
options: Offered wage below the provincial or territorial median
quiz.explanation: LMIA streams often distinguish high-wage and low-wage roles by median wage.

quiz.question: What is a low-wage cap?
options: A limit on the proportion of low-wage temporary foreign workers
quiz.explanation: Low-wage rules can cap TFWs at a worksite.

quiz.question: Why might a medical exam be required?
options: Occupation, residence history, or public health rules can trigger it
quiz.explanation: Medical requirements depend on job type and personal history.

quiz.question: When should a closed-permit holder apply to change employers?
options: Before starting work for the new employer
quiz.explanation: Closed permits are not automatically transferable.

quiz.question: What is a port of entry letter of introduction?
options: A letter supporting permit issuance at entry, not the permit itself
quiz.explanation: The actual permit is issued separately when requirements are met.

quiz.question: Who may need a TRV or eTA besides work permit approval?
options: Workers who require a travel document to board and enter Canada
quiz.explanation: Travel authorization and work authorization are related but separate.

quiz.question: What should an employer avoid in LMIA recruitment?
options: Unfairly tailoring requirements to exclude qualified Canadians
quiz.explanation: Artificially restrictive recruitment can undermine the labour market test.

quiz.question: What is a compliance inspection?
options: A review of whether the employer met program obligations
quiz.explanation: Employers can be inspected for wages, duties, conditions, and records.

quiz.question: Which proof can show the worker is qualified?
options: Resume, credentials, licences, and reference letters
quiz.explanation: The worker must often show they can perform the job.

quiz.question: Why check regulated occupations?
quiz.explanation: Some jobs require registration before the worker can legally perform them.

quiz.question: What is biometrics' role in work permits?
quiz.explanation: Many applicants must provide fingerprints and a photo unless exempt.

quiz.question: Which job change should be addressed before work continues?
options: A substantial change in employer, duties, wage, or location
quiz.explanation: Material changes can make the current permit inaccurate.

quiz.question: What is maintained status for a work permit extension?
options: Temporary status that may continue after a timely extension application
quiz.explanation: Maintained status usually preserves prior conditions while a decision is pending.

quiz.question: What is needed to move from one closed employer to another?
options: A new work permit supported by the new employer's authorization basis
quiz.explanation: Changing closed employers normally requires a new authorization.

quiz.question: What should an LMIA-exempt explanation identify?
options: The exemption code and evidence meeting its criteria
quiz.explanation: LMIA-exempt categories require specific legal criteria.

quiz.question: Why does the employment contract matter?
options: It confirms duties, wage, hours, benefits, and location
quiz.explanation: Contracts help prove offer terms and employer obligations.

quiz.question: What is the usual sequence for TFWP work permits?
options: Employer obtains LMIA, then worker applies for the permit
quiz.explanation: A positive LMIA is usually needed before the worker's application.

quiz.question: What is the risk of working for a different employer on a closed permit?
options: Unauthorized work and future admissibility concerns
quiz.explanation: Closed permits authorize only the listed employment.

quiz.question: Why can employer compliance history affect future hiring?
options: Non-compliant employers may face bans, penalties, or refusal risk
quiz.explanation: Program compliance findings can affect future foreign-worker hiring.

quiz.question: What is the issue with paying less than the approved wage?
options: It may breach employer obligations and permit conditions
quiz.explanation: Employers must generally provide the approved wages and conditions.

quiz.question: When is a duty change problematic?
options: When duties no longer match the approved NOC or offer
quiz.explanation: Material duty changes can require a new assessment.

quiz.question: Why is NOC misclassification risky?
options: It can undermine LMIA, work permit, and later PR claims
quiz.explanation: NOC affects eligibility, wages, duties, and immigration alignment.

quiz.question: What is a risk in owner-operator style LMIA cases?
options: Questions about genuineness, control, and labour market need
quiz.explanation: Ownership-linked employment requires careful evidence.

quiz.question: What weakens an LMIA recruitment file?
options: Rejecting qualified Canadians without documented job-related reasons
quiz.explanation: Recruitment decisions should be legitimate and documented.

quiz.question: Why are layoffs near an LMIA application sensitive?
options: They may raise concerns about labour need and impact on Canadians
quiz.explanation: Recent layoffs can affect whether hiring a foreign worker is justified.

quiz.question: What is the significance of LMIA expiry?
options: The worker must use it for a permit application within validity
quiz.explanation: An LMIA has a validity period for supporting an application.

quiz.question: Which issue can trigger inadmissibility concerns?
options: False documents or undisclosed immigration history
quiz.explanation: Misrepresentation can have serious consequences.

quiz.question: Why distinguish business visitor from worker?
options: Some activities require a work permit while limited business activities may not
quiz.explanation: Misclassifying work as business visits can create unauthorized work.

quiz.question: What is advanced in intra-company transferee cases?
options: Proving corporate relationship and specialized knowledge or executive capacity
quiz.explanation: ICT cases require evidence about both companies and the worker's role.

quiz.question: Why can start-up employers face scrutiny?
options: They must prove they can genuinely employ and pay the worker
quiz.explanation: New businesses may need stronger operational and financial evidence.

quiz.question: What is risky about generic NOC job descriptions?
options: They may fail to prove the actual job is genuine
quiz.explanation: Applications should describe real duties, not copied text alone.

quiz.question: What is central in significant benefit IMP cases?
options: Evidence of important social, cultural, or economic benefit
quiz.explanation: Significant benefit exemptions require persuasive Canada-benefit evidence.

quiz.question: Why can unauthorized work harm later Express Entry plans?
options: It may not count and can raise compliance issues
quiz.explanation: Unauthorized experience can affect both eligibility and credibility.

quiz.question: How should a procedural fairness letter be handled?
options: Address the concerns with evidence before the deadline
quiz.explanation: A PFL is a chance to respond before possible refusal.

quiz.question: Why keep employer records after the worker starts?
options: They may be needed during compliance inspections
quiz.explanation: Employer obligations continue after permit issuance.

quiz.question: Which change may require a fresh LMIA or offer submission?
options: A material change to employer, duties, wage, or location
quiz.explanation: Material changes can require new authorization.

quiz.question: Why do provincial employment standards matter?
options: Employer obligations include applicable workplace laws
quiz.explanation: Foreign-worker programs do not override workplace standards.

quiz.question: What is a risk of inflated recruitment requirements?
options: They may appear designed to exclude local applicants
quiz.explanation: Requirements should be reasonable and necessary.

quiz.question: Why avoid starting before the permit is issued or activated?
options: Approval steps may not yet equal legal authorization to work
quiz.explanation: Work can begin only when the worker has proper authorization.

quiz.question: What supports a reciprocal employment exemption?
options: Evidence of balanced opportunities for Canadians abroad
quiz.explanation: Reciprocity relies on mutual benefit.

quiz.question: What can complicate a work permit extension?
options: Expired passport, changed duties, or employer non-compliance
quiz.explanation: Extensions depend on both worker and employer circumstances.

quiz.question: What is safest when work conditions no longer match the permit?
options: Obtain proper authorization before continuing changed work
quiz.explanation: Correct authorization reduces compliance risk.

donts: Do not proceed with fraudulent employers
Do not submit IRCC before positive LMIA when required
Do not hide unauthorized work history

sampleDocs.title: Sample passport bio page (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Show clients which pages to scan — MRZ visible, no glare, full spread.

sampleDocs.title: Sample job offer / LMIA letter (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Employer name, role, wage, and LMIA number where required.

sampleDocs.title: Sample employer support letter (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Confirms position is genuine and client meets role requirements.

sampleDocs.title: Sample qualifications proof (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Degree and experience matching NOC / SOC role.

sampleDocs.title: Sample bank statement (mock)
sampleDocs.description: 6-month statement with name, account number, and closing balance — explain seasoning.
